growth_records.medium: Defined glucose medium (as in Usai et al., 2020)

up.method: glucose oxidase/peroxidase assay on cell-free supernatant; specific glucose consumption reported (baseline/control) but absolute rate given only graphically (Figures 2A,B)

sec.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H, 5 mM H2SO4, RI/UV 210 nm); control condition
HPLC; yield reported (see yields), absolute titer only graphical (Fig 2A)
HPLC; measured but no numeric value in text
HPLC (RI detector); measured, no significant difference vs butanol condition, no numeric value in text

growth_records.cite: Costa, Paolo et al. Clostridium cellulovorans Proteomic Responses to Butanol Stress. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.674639. [PMC8336468]
growth_records.snippet: formate titer significantly decreased (from 0.30 g/L to 0.07 g/L) ... as well as formate yield (from 0.33 g/g glucose consumed to 0.15 g/g glucose consumed). The opposite pattern was observed for acetate and butyrate yield: ... only 0.13 g/
growth_records.notes: CONTROL (no butanol added) batch condition. Prokaryote (C. cellulovorans 743B). Explicit measured product yields on glucose reported in text (values from Figure 2C): formate 0.33 g/g glucose consumed; acetate 0.13 g/g; butyrate 0.43 g/g. Formate final titer 0.30 g/L. Substrates/products by HPLC; glucose by GOD-POD. Specific growth rate mu was measured (Figure 1B) but numeric value NOT in text -> growth_rate_per_h null. pH not stated. Full medium composition referenced to Usai et al. 2020 (not restated) -> only glucose 5 g/L captured, medium_composition_stated:false. Biomass conversion factor stated: protein/biomass ratio = 0.261 g/g (usable for gDW back-calc if paired with figure data). Lactate and ethanol measured by HPLC but no numeric values given in text. No mmol/gDW/h conversion possible: absolute glucose uptake and biomass-time data are only in figures, not in extractable text.

growth_records.medium: Defined glucose medium (as in Usai et al., 2020) + exogenous n-butanol

up.method: glucose oxidase/peroxidase assay; text states specific glucose consumption was about 2-fold higher than control, but no absolute rate given (Figures 2A,B)

sec.method: HPLC; measured, no numeric value in text
HPLC; measured, no significant difference vs control, no numeric value in text

growth_records.cite: Costa, Paolo et al. Clostridium cellulovorans Proteomic Responses to Butanol Stress. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.674639. [PMC8336468]
growth_records.snippet: Butanol-supplemented cultures showed specific glucose consumption about 2-fold higher than control cultures ... formate titer significantly decreased (from 0.30 g/L to 0.07 g/L) ... formate yield (from 0.33 ... to 0.15 g/g glucose consumed)
growth_records.notes: BUTANOL-CHALLENGED batch condition (6 g/L exogenous n-butanol; butanol is a stressor/inhibitor, NOT a carbon source - <0.35 g/L butanol consumed, i.e. no significant butanol uptake). Explicit measured product yields on glucose (Figure 2C, stated in text): formate 0.15 g/g glucose consumed; acetate 0.29 g/g; butyrate 0.62 g/g. Formate final titer 0.07 g/L. Text: specific glucose consumption ~2-fold higher than control (relative only, no absolute mmol/gDW/h). Specific growth rate mu was measured and butanol lowered it (Figure 1B) but numeric value NOT in text -> null. pH not stated. Full base medium per Usai et al. 2020 (not restated). Butanol growth range assayed 1-8 g/L; 6 g/L was the condition used for proteomics and the definitive product-yield comparison, so captured here. No absolute uptake/secretion rate in mmol/gDW/h derivable from text (biomass-time and titer curves only in figures).
